Why are you interested in this role?: What pulls me to this role is that I genuinely like being the person who keeps the operational engine clean. At InGenius Prep I built the cross-functional processes and documentation for 70+ staff and cut onboarding time by 75%, and the part I enjoyed most wasn't the launch — it was the habit of finding the root cause when a workflow broke and fixing the underlying system so it stayed fixed. A People Systems Coordinator role is that instinct made into a job: treating data integrity as the foundation of a team's trust. And Faire's mission — leveling the playing field for independent retailers — is the kind of work I'd be proud to keep the systems running behind.
Why do you want to work at Faire?: Two reasons, honestly. First, the mission resonates — I grew up around small family businesses, and Faire giving independent retailers a real shot against big-box competition is something I'd be glad to support from the inside. Second, this is exactly the work I do well: I treat People operations as a system to diagnose and improve, not just transactions to process. At Vestas I helped migrate and merge employee records in an M&A with high accuracy and learned how much downstream trust depends on getting the data right the first time. I'd bring that same care to keeping Faire's People systems reliable.
Tell us about yourself / anything else: I'm an analytical organizer with a growth mindset, grounded in an MS in Applied Organizational Psychology from USC. In practice that means I diagnose with data where a process breaks down, collaborate cross-functionally on the fix, and track results to make sure it holds. I'm also genuinely eager to deepen my Workday and workflow-automation skills — I pick up HR platforms quickly and enjoy it. I'd love to bring that careful, systems-minded approach to your People team.
